subject: Re: E Management NY
body:
i need to tell faith re caroline  .. and yuo can call
eddie

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> 2 issues that I would like your direction on with
> respect to E NY's  business
>  
> 1.    Contract for Caroline Poznanski, who JL and 
> Nathalie claim is a "star 
> booker" currently employed by Next.  They want 
> Caroline to terminate her 
> contract with Next and after a stand-still period 
> (non-compete period) provided 
> for in that agreement, have E Management NY employ 
> Caroline.
>  
> ---Issue one.  Does this present a problem/conflict
> vis-a-vis  Faith?  If so, 
> how would you like to proceed?
>  
> --Issue two.    Caroline is fairly certain that, if 
> Caroline moves to ENY, 
> Next will bring suit against Caroline, even if 
> Caroline does nothing wrong.  
> Caroline wants there to be a provision in 
> Caroline's employment agreement with 
> E Management NY that requires E Management  NY to
> indemnify Caroline from 
> liability, costs, etc, arising out of Next  bringing
> a lawsuit against Caroline 
> with respect to Caroline's performance of  her
> employment duties to E 
> Management NY.  Nathalie said that Next sued 
> Nathalie when Nathalie left to go work 
> for Elite.  Nathalie said that the  legal bill
> amounted to approximately 
> $70,000, even though Nathalie ultimately  prevailed
> in the suit.  If Nathalie and 
> Caroline are correct, then E  Management NY is
> effectively buying a lawsuit when 
> it employs Caroline.
>  
> My initial reaction was no indemnity, but JL and
> Caroline claim that it is  
> not uncommon in the industry that the new agency
> indemnifies the booker against 
>  claims by the old agency.  If so, then let's at
> least get a cap on the  
> indemnity.
>  
> Caroline's contract calls for a Salary of $250,000
> per year (no  
> contractually required bonus), as compared with the
> $200,000 Caroline is getting  from 
> Next right now.  The term of the contract with E NY
> is 1 year subject  to auto 
> renewal unless either party declines renewal 3
> months before the  scheduled end 
> of the term or unless either party terminates for
> cause. 
>  
> Essentially then, we are buying an expensive lawsuit
> for one year of  
> contract service.
>  
>  
> 2.    MORE SERIOUS AND  PROBLEMATIC
>  
> I received the following e-mail from John Luc and a
> telephone call from  
> Nathalie about the same:
>  
>
++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
>  
>  
> The webmaster has made a mistake on  the printing
> and some pacages have been 
> sent with the  tittle 
> ELITE MODELS   NEW  York 
> Deep  SHâ€¦â€¦. 
> I was thinking to call Eddy tTrump  now and explain
> him\ 
> We will send the letter from the Web  master who
> took the responsibility and 
> send a corrective made in common with E  Trump 
> What do you  think??
> 
>
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
>  
> Not  good.  Apparently, packages regarding ENY's
> models were transmitted to  
> ENY's clients over the internet with an incorrect
> source identifier indicating 
>  that the packages came from Nathalie at "Elite
> Models New York."  Clearly  
> this is no way to stay off Eddie Trump's radar while
> we move  forward.
>  
> My initial inclination is not to contact Eddie, but
> to send out to all of  
> those who received the packages with the erroneous
> Elite Models New York  
> identifier a notice from E Management NY correcting
> the error and clearly  stating 
> that E NY and its models have no affiliation with
> Elite USA and include  in 
> that notice a letter from the Web Master stating
> that the incorrect usage of  the 
> Elite Models name was through no fault of ENY who
> correctly identified the  
> source of the packages as being from ENY and,
> without ENY's knowledge, the  
> packages were misidentified solely as a result of
> the Web Master's programming  
> error, which the Web Master is now correcting.  
>  
> Why unnecessarily bring this to Eddie's attention,
> when we are taking the  
> very steps necessary to correct the problem and
> limit industry confusion as to  
> affiliation between Elite and ENY.
>  
> On the other hand, on the theory (I have no idea if
> it is true or not) that  
> there is no way to keep Eddie Trump in the dark
> about what we are doing or 
> what  happened in this case, would it be prudent to
> contact Eddie and show him 
> the  notice and corrective letter we are intending
> to send out in order to 
> soften his  reaction?
>  
> Your thoughts?
>  
> Darren K.  Indyke
> Attorney-At-Law
> 457 Madison Avenue
> 4th Floor
> New York, New  York 10022
> Tel: 212-750-1176
> Fax: 212-750-0381
> e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 
> 
> 
> Confidentiality  Notice: This communication and any
> attachments contain 
> information from Darren  K. Indyke, Esq. that
> constitute attorney work product or 
> that are otherwise  confidential and/or privileged.
> Except for personal use by 
> the intended  recipient, or as expressly authorized
> by the sender, any person 
> who receives  this information is prohibited from
> disclosing, copying, 
> distributing, and/or  using it. If you have received
> this communication in error, 
> please immediately  delete it and all copies, and
> promptly notify the sender at 
> the above telephone  number or electronic mail
> address. Nothing in this 
> communication is intended to  operate as an
> electronic signature under applicable  
> law.
>

body_html:
i need to tell faith re caroline .. and yuo can call
eddie

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

2 issues that I would like your direction on with
respect to E NY's business

  1. Contract for Caroline Poznanski, who JL and

Nathalie claim is a "star
booker" currently employed by Next. They want
Caroline to terminate her
contract with Next and after a stand-still period
(non-compete period) provided
for in that agreement, have E Management NY employ
Caroline.

---Issue one. Does this present a problem/conflict
vis-a-vis Faith? If so,
how would you like to proceed?

--Issue two. Caroline is fairly certain that, if
Caroline moves to ENY,
Next will bring suit against Caroline, even if
Caroline does nothing wrong.
Caroline wants there to be a provision in
Caroline's employment agreement with
E Management NY that requires E Management NY to
indemnify Caroline from
liability, costs, etc, arising out of Next bringing
a lawsuit against Caroline
with respect to Caroline's performance of her
employment duties to E
Management NY. Nathalie said that Next sued
Nathalie when Nathalie left to go work
for Elite. Nathalie said that the legal bill
amounted to approximately
$70,000, even though Nathalie ultimately prevailed
in the suit. If Nathalie and
Caroline are correct, then E Management NY is
effectively buying a lawsuit when
it employs Caroline.

My initial reaction was no indemnity, but JL and
Caroline claim that it is
not uncommon in the industry that the new agency
indemnifies the booker against
claims by the old agency. If so, then let's at
least get a cap on the
indemnity.

Caroline's contract calls for a Salary of $250,000
per year (no
contractually required bonus), as compared with the
$200,000 Caroline is getting from
Next right now. The term of the contract with E NY
is 1 year subject to auto
renewal unless either party declines renewal 3
months before the scheduled end
of the term or unless either party terminates for
cause.

Essentially then, we are buying an expensive lawsuit
for one year of
contract service.

  1. MORE SERIOUS AND PROBLEMATIC

I received the following e-mail from John Luc and a
telephone call from
Nathalie about the same:

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

The webmaster has made a mistake on the printing
and some pacages have been
sent with the tittle
ELITE MODELS NEW York
Deep SHâ€¦â€¦.
I was thinking to call Eddy tTrump now and explain
him\
We will send the letter from the Web master who
took the responsibility and
send a corrective made in common with E Trump
What do you think??

+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Not good. Apparently, packages regarding ENY's
models were transmitted to
ENY's clients over the internet with an incorrect
source identifier indicating
that the packages came from Nathalie at "Elite
Models New York." Clearly
this is no way to stay off Eddie Trump's radar while
we move forward.

My initial inclination is not to contact Eddie, but
to send out to all of
those who received the packages with the erroneous
Elite Models New York
identifier a notice from E Management NY correcting
the error and clearly stating
that E NY and its models have no affiliation with
Elite USA and include in
that notice a letter from the Web Master stating
that the incorrect usage of the
Elite Models name was through no fault of ENY who
correctly identified the
source of the packages as being from ENY and,
without ENY's knowledge, the
packages were misidentified solely as a result of
the Web Master's programming
error, which the Web Master is now correcting.

Why unnecessarily bring this to Eddie's attention,
when we are taking the
very steps necessary to correct the problem and
limit industry confusion as to
affiliation between Elite and ENY.

On the other hand, on the theory (I have no idea if
it is true or not) that
there is no way to keep Eddie Trump in the dark
about what we are doing or
what happened in this case, would it be prudent to
contact Eddie and show him
the notice and corrective letter we are intending
to send out in order to
soften his reaction?

Your thoughts?

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com)

Confidentiality Notice: This communication and any

promptly notify the sender at

address. Nothing in this
communication is intended to operate as an
electronic signature under applicable
